topics: States of Water: Solid, Liquid and Gas
Condensation: Water Vapour Becoming Water Again
subject: Science

sections.heading: ⚔️ The Quest Begins: Ice and Water Are the Same Hero!
sections.body: Imagine your shikanji on a hot summer day. The ice in your glass feels hard and cold. The water feels wet and flows freely. They seem totally different, right? But here is the magical truth — ice and water are actually the SAME substance, just in different forms called states! When you take ice out of the freezer and leave it on a plate, it slowly turns into water. No wizard added anything — it just changed its state all by itself!
keyPoints: Ice is the solid state of water and water is the liquid state — same substance, different forms
Water has three states: solid (ice), liquid (water), and gas (water vapour)
Water flows and splashes, but ice is hard and stays in one place

sections.heading: 🔥 The Disappearing Spell: What is Evaporation?
sections.body: After rain, you see big puddles in your school playground. But by evening, they are gone! Where did all that water go? It did not seep through the ground fully — it turned into invisible water vapour and went up into the air. This power is called evaporation. Think about when your mother makes dosa — she sprinkles water on the hot tawa and WHOOSH, it vanishes into steam! Even your wet clothes drying on a rope or sweat drying off your skin after cricket — that is all evaporation doing its magic!
keyPoints: Evaporation is when liquid water changes into water vapour (gas) and goes into the air
It happens even at room temperature — not just on hot surfaces
Drying clothes, mopped floors, and puddles disappearing are all examples of evaporation

sections.heading: 💧 The Hidden Realm of Water Vapour in Air
sections.body: Here is a secret — the air around us always has invisible water vapour floating in it. You cannot see it, but it is there like a hidden dragon in the realm of the sky! When you make dosa, steam rises up. That steam looks white because it has tiny water droplets mixed with water vapour. But pure water vapour itself is totally invisible. The air holds this water vapour quietly until something cold comes along and reveals it!
keyPoints: Water vapour is the gas form of water and is invisible to our eyes
Steam looks white because it contains tiny visible water droplets mixed with water vapour
Air around us always contains some amount of water vapour

sections.heading: 🧊 The Condensation Spell: Vapour Turns Back to Water!
sections.body: Now here is the most exciting part of our quest! When you take a cold glass of water with ice cubes, you will notice tiny water droplets forming on the OUTSIDE of the glass. Where did they come from? The glass did not leak! The answer is that water vapour floating in the warm air around the glass touched the cold surface and — ZAP — it turned back into liquid water droplets. This is called condensation. You see this same magic every morning on leaves — those are dew drops! Vapour in the cool night air touched the cold leaves and became water droplets.
keyPoints: Condensation is when water vapour in air touches a cold surface and turns into liquid water droplets
Water droplets on a cold glass, dew on leaves, and drops on a pot lid are all condensation
Condensation is the opposite of evaporation — it is the reverse journey of water

sections.heading: 🏆 Mastery Unlocked: The Full Journey of Water
sections.body: Water is the greatest shape-shifter in nature — a true wizard of the natural world! It travels from solid ice to flowing liquid to invisible gas and back again. When the sun heats up a puddle, evaporation happens and water goes up into the sky as vapour. When that vapour meets something cold, condensation happens and water comes back down as droplets. This is how clouds form and how rain falls — the whole water cycle is powered by these two spells: evaporation and condensation. As the Thirukkural wisely says, even the mighty ocean can dry up if it does not rain — that is how precious water truly is!
keyPoints: Evaporation and condensation are opposite processes that keep water cycling through nature
The water cycle — puddles, clouds, and rain — is powered by these two states changes
Water is precious; we must understand it to protect it

summary: In this quest, we discovered that water is a shape-shifting hero that exists in three states — solid ice, liquid water, and invisible gas called water vapour. Ice and water are the same substance, just in different forms. When water heats up and turns into vapour and escapes into the air, that process is called evaporation — it is why puddles vanish, clothes dry, and sweat disappears from our skin. The air around us always carries invisible water vapour, and when that vapour touches a cold surface like an ice-cold glass or a leaf on a cool morning, it turns back into water droplets through a process called condensation. These two magical spells — evaporation and condensation — work together to keep water journeying through nature, forming clouds, bringing rain, and making life possible on Earth!
